summary: Reviewed the guest progress interpolation change and host challenge-song detection change for unhappy-path behavior. The main risks are guest-side reveal state becoming non-authoritative during track transitions/extensions and challenge detection over-classifying tracks after ID matching fails. Overall the patch needs targeted fixes before merge because it can expose song answers or show the wrong live-game state to guests.
appears_sound: The guest interpolation interval is cleaned up with `window.clearInterval`, so the new timer does not persist after unmount or dependency changes in `app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:60`.
The host still gives intro songs precedence by forcing `isChallengeSong` to false before any challenge detection in `app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:339`.

findings.description: When a track changes, the previous track's `tick` value is reused for the first render of the new track before the reset effect runs. Failure sequence: track A has accumulated 20+ ticks, host advances to track B at progress 0, guest renders `0 + oldTick * 1000`, and the new song's title/artist or advance state can flash on the projection before being hidden again.
findings.evidence: `tick` is reset in an effect at `app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:51`, but render uses `anchor.progress + tick * 1000` at `app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:64`, and React effects run after paint.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The flash may be brief, but a projection screen revealing an answer even for one paint is still visible user impact.

findings.description: Guest reveal state now ignores host extension timing. Failure sequence: host extends the current track, host computes `nextMs` with `extensionMs`, guest recomputes from the base session/challenge config, and the guest screen can show “Advancing to next song...” while the host intentionally keeps the song active.
findings.evidence: Guest computes `localRevealState` from `interpolatedProgress` and `effectiveCfg` in `app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:199`, while host applies `extensionMs` to `baseCfg.nextMs` in `app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:343`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If extension is never used in the live workflow, users may not hit this path, but the host code clearly supports it.

findings.description: Challenge detection now falls back to broad text matching even when the resolved challenge-ID set exists and the current track ID is not in it. Failure sequence: a non-challenge song shares title/artist substrings with a challenge song, ID check fails, text fallback returns true, and the host/guest apply challenge reveal timing to the wrong track.
findings.evidence: `detectChallenge` checks `challengeTrackIdsRef` first but always returns `matchesChallengeSong(t, game)` on ID miss at `app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:337`; the matcher uses bidirectional substring checks at `app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:53`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The fallback may be intentional to rescue unresolved Spotify IDs, but the current code cannot distinguish unresolved challenge songs from resolved non-challenge tracks.
findings.what_would_confirm: Test with a loaded playlist where a non-challenge track title/artist contains a configured challenge song title/artist while its Spotify ID is absent from `challengeTrackIdsRef`.

empty_categories: No new server-side write, retry, transaction, or idempotency paths were introduced in this diff.
No new external API timeout or error-handling branches were added by the changed code.
No unmount cleanup issue found for the new guest interpolation interval.
